_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize TX House HD89-93 batch (noble, romero, lowe, bhojani, schatzline). State tier. Cited. Schatzline retiring (not_running).

profile.background: HD89 (Collin County: Allen, Murphy, Wylie, Lucas, Princeton, part of Plano). In office since 2019; Vice Chair Ways & Means property-tax subcommittee. Former TX Juvenile Justice Board appointee. Won 2026 GOP primary; on Nov 2026 ballot.

candy-noble@TX.notes_set: Strong social-conservative record. Texas Alliance for Life 96% (89th), Texas Right to Life 66% (2023), Texas Values Action 100% 'Faith & Family Champion'. Sponsored SB33 (no taxpayer-funded abortion travel), co-authored SB8 Heartbeat + trigger ban, authored HB1929 (bar public funds to abortion providers). Supported HB229 'What is a Woman Act', SB1257 detransition, SB12 Parents' Bill of Rights, SB13 library books, SB10 Ten Commandments, SB11 school prayer, SB965 Coach Kennedy; authored SB1515 (Ten Commandments display). Voted SB2 school choice. Voted FOR HB1927 constitutional carry (2021), NRA 'A'/TSRA endorsed. Co-authored $6B+ border security funding. Weak spot: Texans for Fiscal Responsibility career grade F. Also voted to impeach AG Paxton (2023) — intra-GOP establishment split, not progressive. Election/justice/federal-overreach specific roll calls not independently confirmed -> nulled.

0.note: Affirms life: 'never support ending of innocent life under any circumstance'; TAL 96%.

1.note: Restriction-focused (heartbeat, funding bans); no explicit abolition advocacy found.

2.note: No public position on embryonic research/IVF discard.

3.note: No public position on euthanasia/assisted suicide found.

4.note: Authored HB1929 to defund Planned Parenthood; pro-life champion, no abortion-industry funding.

4.note: Opposed HB1738 (sodomy-statute repeal); supported SB12 Parents' Bill of Rights and SB13 library-books.

NRA 'A'/TSRA endorsed; opposes most gun restrictions.

profile.background: HD90 (Fort Worth, Tarrant County). In office since 2015; first Latino to represent Tarrant County in the Legislature; Chair of the Mexican American Legislative Caucus since 2024. Small-business owner (tile/stone supply). Unopposed in 2026 Democratic primary; on Nov 2026 ballot.

ramon-romero-jr@TX.notes_set: Conventional progressive Democrat record (the 'pro-life Democrat' premise is refuted by the data). Texas Alliance for Life 48% (89th), Texas Right to Life negative ratings, Texas Values Action 0% (2025)/9% (2023). Voted AGAINST SB8 (Women's Privacy), SB33 (abortion-travel funding), HB229 'What is a Woman Act', SB1257 detransition, SB10 Ten Commandments, SB11 school prayer, SB965 Coach Kennedy, SB2 school choice, SB13 library books; voted FOR HB1738 (sodomy-statute repeal). On immigration ran a 3-day hunger strike against SB4 (anti-sanctuary, 2017) and joined the 2025 quorum break. Economic/election/justice/federal/gun individual roll calls not documented -> nulled (SB14 88th, HB1927 87th, SB1 87th could not be confirmed at primary-source level). No cross-party deviation found.

1.note: Voted against pro-life priority bills (SB8/SB33) — opposite of abolition.

2.note: No position on embryonic research found.

4.note: No evidence of abortion-industry funding located -> not inferred.

2.note: Voted against HB229 'What is a Woman Act' and SB1257 detransition.

3.note: No position on no-fault divorce found.

profile.background: HD91 (Tarrant County: Haltom City, North Richland Hills, Richland Hills, Watauga, NE Fort Worth). Army Military Police veteran (~2003-2015, two Afghanistan combat tours); later peace officer/corrections. Defeated incumbent Stephanie Klick in the 2024 GOP primary; first session is the 89th (2025). Won 2026 GOP primary (~63%); on Nov 2026 ballot vs Yisak Worku (D).

david-lowe@TX.notes_set: Hardline/Freedom-Caucus-aligned freshman. Texas Right to Life 75, Texas Alliance for Life 74% (89th), Texans for Fiscal Responsibility Fiscal Index 98 (2025), NRA-endorsed, Texas Values-endorsed. Self-described pro-life abolitionist ('Abortion is murder'; wants an 'abolish abortion bill'). Voted FOR SB2 (school choice) and to table Talarico voucher amendment. Supported prior HB68 (classifying child gender modification as child abuse). Anti-federal: 'I don't even like the US Department of Education', 'ban vaccine mandates.' Opposes red-flag laws ('gateway to unconstitutional gun confiscation'). Campaign priorities: phase out property taxes, secure border. NOTE: he did NOT author the 'Texas Sovereignty Act' (HB796 is Cecil Bell's) -> that cell nulled. SB10/SB11 individual votes, NFA, castle doctrine not located -> nulled.

0.note: 'Abortion is murder'; 'unborn children should be protected under the 14th amendment'; TRTL 75/TAL 74%.

profile.background: HD92 (Tarrant County: Euless/Irving area). Pakistan-born attorney; former Euless City Council member; among the first Muslims and first South Asian in the Texas Legislature, sworn Jan 2023. Unopposed in 2026 Democratic primary; on Nov 2026 ballot vs JP Woodruff (R).

salman-bhojani@TX.notes_set: Progressive Democrat record. Texas Values Action 5% (89th). Pro-choice ('I trust Texans to make their own decisions... including whether or not to have an abortion'); endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes (2024); voted against SB8, SB33. Voted FOR HB1738 (sodomy-statute repeal); against HB229 'What is a Woman Act', SB1257 detransition. Voted NO on SB2 school choice; against SB12 Parents' Bill of Rights, SB13 library books. Voted against SB10 Ten Commandments, SB11 school prayer, SB965 Coach Kennedy — stating Ten Commandments display 'sends a message of confusion... religious freedom... applies to all faith traditions, not just one.' Opposed SB147 (foreign land-ownership ban). Authored a bill barring school exams on religious holidays (incl. Islamic). Economic/election/justice/gun votes not documented -> nulled. No cross-party deviation found.

profile.religion: Christian (pastor, Mercy Culture Church, Fort Worth)
profile.background: HD93 (Tarrant County: Fort Worth/Haslet). Pastor and former anti-trafficking nonprofit director; in office since Jan 2023. RETIRING: announced 2025-10-27 he will not seek a third term, leaving to join the National Faith Advisory Board; not on the Nov 2026 ballot (Alan Blaylock won the 2026 GOP primary for the open seat).

nate-schatzline@TX.notes_set: Strong social-conservative record; retiring after the current term (announced 2025-10-27, not on 2026 ballot). Texas Right to Life 100, Texas Alliance for Life 85% (89th), Texas Values Action 100% 'Faith & Family Champion', Texans for Fiscal Responsibility Fiscal Index 96 (2025). Voted FOR SB33, SCR19 (Life Monument), SB1233. Authored HB1266 (restricting sexually oriented/drag performances accessible to minors). Voted FOR HB229 'What is a Woman Act', SB1257 detransition; AGAINST HB1738 repeal. Voted FOR SB2 school choice, SB12 Parents' Bill of Rights, SB13 library books, HB100; authored a 2025 bill making bookstores liable for selling obscene content to minors. Voted FOR SB10 Ten Commandments, SB11 school prayer, SB965 Coach Kennedy (supporter, not lead author). Election/justice/federal/border/gun specifics not documented this pass -> nulled.
